behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this is deterministic formula evaluation logic over in-memory inputs. Downgrade check: no lower level exists. Upgrade check rejected: behavior text does not require module handoff, disk, subprocess, network, or AI output. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by the behavior being a single formula computation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the formula evaluator on a prepared in-memory cell context with A1..A10 values (including edge values like negatives/decimals) and assert exact SUM result.

behaviors.description: `=B1+1` where `B1` is `=A1*2` yields the correct cascaded value.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because failure appears at module boundary between reference resolution and formula evaluation across cells. Downgrade check rejected: pure single-function test can miss handoff/state bugs between dependency resolver and evaluator. Upgrade check rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess/AI dependency stated.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-memory sheet with A1, B1 formula, and dependent cell formula; run recalculation and assert final value reflects chained dependency evaluation.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1->B1, B1->A1) returns a circular-reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because cycle detection depends on cross-cell dependency graph plus evaluator error propagation. Downgrade check rejected: a unit-only cycle utility test may not catch engine-level non-termination/error-surfacing behavior. Upgrade check rejected: no real infrastructure or AI dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create two in-memory cells with mutual references, trigger evaluation, assert circular-reference error is returned quickly and no repeated-evaluation loop occurs.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact-match returns correct value and missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this is deterministic built-in function semantics. Downgrade check: no lower level exists. Upgrade check rejected: behavior text does not require inter-module wiring or real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke VLOOKUP function evaluator with in-memory table ranges: one case with exact key hit and one miss; assert returned value/error codes.

behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because correctness is local expression evaluation and branching semantics. Downgrade check: no lower level exists. Upgrade check rejected: no evidence of required module boundary, OS, or AI behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` with A1 values spanning branches (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, as this is local string-composition behavior of one built-in function. Downgrade check: no lower level exists. Upgrade check rejected: no real infra/module-chain requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run CONCATENATE evaluator with inputs containing normal text and empty cells; assert separator placement and omission of empties.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference `=Sheet2!A1+1` resolves when referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because failure is at boundary between sheet lookup/name resolution and expression evaluation. Downgrade check rejected: a single-function arithmetic test will miss cross-sheet resolution wiring issues. Upgrade check rejected: no OS/network/subprocess/AI dependency stated.
behaviors.test_description: Build an in-memory workbook with two sheets, set Sheet2!A1, evaluate formula on Sheet1, and assert resolved value uses the other sheet cell.

behaviors.description: Changing a referenced cell triggers dependent recalculation in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this behavior targets interaction of dependency tracking, invalidation, and recomputation ordering. Downgrade check rejected: unit-testing one sorter or evaluator function can miss cross-module propagation bugs. Integration brake: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create dependency chain/diamond in-memory, change a source cell, run recompute, and assert dependents are updated to values consistent with topological recomputation.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because failure boundary is between undo/redo history, formula storage, and recalculation state restoration. Downgrade check rejected: unit-testing undo stack alone misses dependent-value restoration integration failures. Upgrade check rejected: no required external OS/network/subprocess or AI output quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In-memory workbook: set formula and dependents, edit formula, confirm new values, perform undo, then assert formula text and all dependent computed values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to custom binary format and reload with identical formulas/values and no floating-point precision change.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is serializer/deserializer + engine state round-trip with local filesystem. Downgrade check rejected: unit tests on serializer functions alone can miss end-to-end workbook reconstruction mismatches. Upgrade check considered and rejected: behavior requires local disk round-trip but not subprocess/network/OS-event behavior; by level defs, local filesystem fits Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Write a workbook with representative formulas and floating-point values to a temp file using real save API, reload via load API, and assert formula text plus numeric values are preserved within strict precision criteria.
